tested_steps: Ran `make test` and confirmed 1512 unit tests passed.
Ran `npx playwright test tests/e2e/smoke.spec.ts` and confirmed 11 smoke tests passed.
Ran `make db-push` against RDS Postgres and confirmed Drizzle completed with `No changes detected`.

Verified the live database contains the expected public tables: organizations, user, org_memberships, projects, deployments, pages, api_keys, agent_jobs, assistant_conversations, analytics_events, and audit_logs.

Inserted parent and child records in a transaction, deleted the organization, and confirmed cascading deletes removed memberships, projects, pages, deployments, API keys, agent jobs, assistant conversations, analytics events, and audit logs.
Verified unique indexes reject duplicate organization slugs and duplicate org memberships with Postgres error code `23505`.
Verified enum columns reject invalid `organizations.plan` and `deployments.status` values with Postgres error code `22P02`.

Verified default JSON/JSONB values are correct in practice: organizations.settings `{}`, projects.settings `{}`, pages.frontmatter `{}`, agent_jobs.messages `[]`, assistant_conversations.messages `[]`, analytics_events.data `{}`, and audit_logs.details `{}`.
Ran `make test` and confirmed the full unit suite passed before deeper QA.
Ran `npx playwright test tests/e2e/smoke.spec.ts` and confirmed smoke coverage still passed.
Confirmed an unauthenticated `POST /api/uploads/presign` and `GET /api/uploads/presign` returned live S3 presigned URLs, reproducing a storage auth bypass before the fix.
Added route-level auth + org membership enforcement and verified the same unauthenticated POST now returns `401 Unauthorized`.

Generated a real Better Auth session cookie tied to a dedicated QA org/project in Postgres, then requested an authorized upload presign and confirmed the URL encoded a 900 second (`X-Amz-Expires=900`) expiry window.
Uploaded a real text file to S3 via the presigned PUT URL and verified the authorized download presign returned the uploaded `hello infra002` payload.

Verified real bucket CORS with an `OPTIONS` preflight to the presigned URL: `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: http://localhost:3015`, methods `GET, PUT, POST`, and `Access-Control-Allow-Headers: content-type`.
Requested a 49 MiB upload presign, uploaded the file successfully, and confirmed the stored S3 object content length was `51380224` bytes with content type `video/mp4`.
Requested an upload larger than 50 MiB and verified the app rejected it with `400 Bad Request` and `File exceeds maximum size of 52428800 bytes`.
Requested a presign for a 5 byte upload and attempted to PUT a 10 byte body; S3 rejected it with `403 SignatureDoesNotMatch`, confirming the signed content length is enforced.

Requested two presigned URLs for the same key, uploaded `AAAAAA` and `BBBBBB` concurrently, and verified both writes succeeded with the final stored object resolving to one winner (`BBBBBB` in this run), i.e. last-write-wins overwrite semantics with no object lock.

bugs_found.description: The uploads presign API minted real S3 upload and download URLs for unauthenticated callers and for arbitrary org/project identifiers.
bugs_found.expected: Only authenticated members of the target project's organization should be able to mint upload/download presigned URLs, and only admins/editors should be allowed to upload new assets.
bugs_found.actual: Anonymous requests received fully usable presigned S3 URLs for both PUT and GET operations.
bugs_found.reproduction: POST `http://localhost:3015/api/uploads/presign` with any orgId/projectId/filename/contentType body, or GET `/api/uploads/presign?key=...`, without cookies.

bugs_found.description: The advertised 50 MB upload cap was only enforced in the client and the presigned URL did not bind the upload size.
bugs_found.expected: Oversized uploads should be rejected server-side and the presigned PUT should enforce the expected content length.
bugs_found.actual: Before the fix, the route accepted any size because `size` was not validated and the signed request omitted `content-length`.
bugs_found.reproduction: Request a presigned URL without a size field and upload any body length directly to S3.

fix_description: Locked `/api/uploads/presign` behind authenticated project access, restricted uploads to admin/editor roles, required a `size` field, and signed `Content-Length` so real S3 uploads enforce the 50 MB limit.

tested_steps: Added the missing `react-is` dependency, which was preventing the Next.js dev server from compiling and returning `/login` successfully.
Ran `make test` and confirmed the unit suite passed with 1521 tests.
Ran `npx playwright test tests/e2e/smoke.spec.ts` and confirmed 11 smoke tests passed, including the authenticated dashboard, settings, editor, analytics, workflows, and MCP pages.

Used Ever CLI on `http://localhost:3015/login` to verify the real Google OAuth flow with `ashleyha0317@gmail.com`: login page rendered, Google account selection worked, consent completed, and the callback redirected into `/onboarding` for the first-time user path.
Queried Postgres after the real OAuth callback and confirmed Better Auth created `user`, `account`, and `session` records for `ashleyha0317@gmail.com` with provider `google` and a live session row.
Called `/api/auth/get-session` from the authenticated browser session and confirmed the response returned the live Better Auth session and Google user payload.

Refreshed the authenticated onboarding page and confirmed the session persisted; navigating to `/login` and `/dashboard` in the same browser session kept the user authenticated and routed back into the authenticated flow.

Signed out through `POST /api/auth/sign-out`, confirmed `/api/auth/get-session` returned `null`, confirmed the session row was removed from Postgres, and confirmed `/dashboard` redirected back to `/login?returnTo=%2Fdashboard`.
Verified the test-only `POST /api/test/create-session` route returned `404 Not Found` in normal dev mode, so the Playwright auth helper remains guarded outside test runs.

bugs_found.description: The app could not compile in dev because `recharts` imported `react-is`, but `react-is` was not installed.
bugs_found.expected: Auth pages should load successfully so the Google OAuth flow can be exercised end to end.
bugs_found.actual: Navigating to `/login` returned a Next.js `500` error caused by `Module not found: Can't resolve 'react-is'` from `src/app/analytics/page.tsx`.
bugs_found.reproduction: Start the dev server with `npm run dev` and request `http://localhost:3015/login`; Next.js fails compilation because `recharts` cannot resolve `react-is`.

fix_description: Installed the missing `react-is` dependency, then re-ran auth QA to verify real Google OAuth, DB-backed session creation, persistence, sign-out, protected-route redirects, and the test-only session route guard.

tested_steps: Ran `make test` and confirmed the unit suite passed with 1521 tests.
Ran `npx playwright test tests/e2e/smoke.spec.ts` and confirmed the 11 smoke tests passed.
Ran `npx playwright test tests/e2e/onboarding.spec.ts` and confirmed 12 onboarding tests passed, including empty-slug validation, existing-org redirect, and concurrent org creation.

Used Ever CLI against `http://localhost:3015/login` with the real Google OAuth account `ashleyha0317@gmail.com`; Google redirected back into the clone and, with no org membership present, the authenticated user landed on `/onboarding`.

Completed the live onboarding flow in Ever: created organization `Auth 002 QA Org`, skipped the optional GitHub step, created project `Auth 002 QA Project`, reached the success screen, and then landed on the dashboard.

Queried Postgres after the live flow and confirmed the real OAuth user had an `admin` membership for org slug `auth-002-qa-org`, plus project slug `auth-002-qa-project` with subdomain `auth-002-qa-org-auth-002-qa-project`.
Navigated the authenticated browser back to `/onboarding` after org creation and confirmed it redirected to `/dashboard`, satisfying the existing-org redirect path.

Started a temporary `PLAYWRIGHT_TEST=true` dev server, created two isolated authenticated test sessions through `/api/test/create-session`, posted the same org name twice to `/api/orgs`, and confirmed slug collision handling returned `slug-collision-org` and `slug-collision-org-mnqomz3k`.
Ran `make test` and confirmed the unit suite passed with 1521 tests before deeper QA.
Ran the smoke suite against the active dev server and confirmed all 11 smoke tests passed.

Used Ever CLI on `http://localhost:3015/settings/organization/api-keys` with the real Google-authenticated session to verify the page renders, create an assistant key through the modal, confirm the one-time reveal, confirm copy-to-clipboard flips to `Copied!`, and confirm revoke removes the key from the list. Saved screenshots to `ralph/screenshots/qa/auth-003-created-key.jpg` and `ralph/screenshots/qa/auth-003-after-revoke.jpg`.

Created a real admin API key and a real assistant API key for org `auth-002-qa-org`, then verified the admin key successfully authenticated `POST /api/v1/project/update/3c9de81a-cca7-4673-aaf5-29bc69aea5f7` with `201 Created` and a deployment status id.

Verified assistant-route auth against the live server: before the fix, `POST /api/v1/assistant/search` returned `500 Internal Server Error` for both a valid assistant key and a valid admin key; after the fix, the same assistant key returned `200 OK` and the admin key returned `403 Forbidden ‚Äî assistant API key required`.
Queried Postgres after the live API requests and confirmed `api_keys.last_used_at` updated for the keys that successfully authenticated.

Demoted the real org membership in Postgres from `admin` to `editor` and then `viewer`, and from the still-authenticated browser session verified both roles received `403 Forbidden ‚Äî admin role required` on `POST /api/api-keys` and `DELETE /api/api-keys/:id`. Restored the membership to `admin` afterward.

Issued 10 concurrent `POST /api/api-keys` requests in the same org and confirmed all 10 returned `201 Created` with distinct `mint_` key prefixes, so no collision or race failure occurred in this run.
Cleaned up the QA-created API keys from the live org after verification.
Added authenticated Playwright coverage for the assistant-key auth contract and re-ran `tests/e2e/api-keys.spec.ts`, which passed 7/7 after updating stale assertions to match the current UI contract.
Ran `make check && make test` after the fix and confirmed typecheck, lint/format, and the full unit suite passed.

bugs_found.description: Assistant API routes returned `500 Internal Server Error` for valid assistant keys instead of serving the request.
bugs_found.expected: A valid `mint_dsc_` assistant key should authenticate assistant routes and return a normal JSON response, even when the search result set is empty.
bugs_found.actual: A real `POST /api/v1/assistant/search` with a valid assistant key updated `last_used_at` but failed with an empty `500` response.
bugs_found.reproduction: Create a real assistant API key from `/settings/organization/api-keys`, then `curl -X POST http://localhost:3015/api/v1/assistant/search -H 'Authorization: Bearer <assistant-key>' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"query":"getting started","pageSize":3}'`.

bugs_found.description: Assistant-only API routes accepted admin keys.
bugs_found.expected: Routes documented as requiring assistant API keys should reject `mint_` admin keys with `403 Forbidden`.
bugs_found.actual: Before the fix, a real `mint_` admin key authenticated assistant routes and reached the route logic instead of being rejected.
bugs_found.reproduction: Create a real admin API key from `/settings/organization/api-keys`, then `curl -X POST http://localhost:3015/api/v1/assistant/search -H 'Authorization: Bearer <admin-key>' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"query":"getting started","pageSize":3}'`.

fix_description: Replaced the assistant-route project scoping query with `inArray(...)`, enforced assistant-only key type checks across the assistant v1 routes, and tightened the API-key E2E coverage to exercise real assistant/admin bearer-key behavior.

tested_steps: Ran `make test` and confirmed the unit suite passed with 1527 tests.
Ran `npx playwright test tests/e2e/smoke.spec.ts` and confirmed 11 smoke tests passed against the authenticated dashboard routes.

Used Ever CLI with the real Google-authenticated browser session on `http://localhost:3015` to verify the dashboard shell on `/dashboard`, `/settings/project/general`, and `/products/agent`, including the shared sidebar/top bar layout and active navigation states. Saved a reference screenshot to `ralph/screenshots/qa/design-001-dashboard-shell.jpg`.

Verified the shell state behaviors manually in Ever: sidebar collapse persisted across navigation via `localStorage`, theme selection persisted across route changes, the trial banner dismissed and stayed dismissed after navigation, and the org/project dropdown exposed the current workspace plus project selection actions.

Re-ran `tests/e2e/dashboard-layout.spec.ts` and confirmed all 16 layout-shell checks passed, including profile navigation, theme persistence, trial banner persistence, mobile off-canvas sidebar behavior, and logout redirect handling.
Re-ran `tests/e2e/settings-layout.spec.ts` and confirmed all 7 settings-shell checks passed, verifying the settings area shares the same dashboard shell.

bugs_found.description: The dashboard layout E2E spec treated the Radix profile-menu item as a `link`, so the profile navigation check timed out even though the menu item was rendered and clickable as a `menuitem`.
bugs_found.expected: The test should target the actual accessible role emitted by the profile menu so the workspace-profile navigation is verified correctly.
bugs_found.actual: Playwright waited 30 seconds for `getByRole('link', { name: 'Your profile' })` even though the visible control had role `menuitem`.
bugs_found.reproduction: Run `npx playwright test tests/e2e/dashboard-layout.spec.ts` before the fix and observe the timeout in `profile menu links to the workspace profile page`.

bugs_found.description: The dashboard layout E2E spec executed the logout assertion before later shell checks, invalidating the shared auth state and producing false login-page failures for theme, banner, greeting, and mobile tests.
bugs_found.expected: The logout case should run after the rest of the authenticated shell assertions, or otherwise use isolated auth state.
bugs_found.actual: After the logout test deleted the shared session, subsequent tests loaded `/login` instead of the dashboard shell.
bugs_found.reproduction: Run `npx playwright test tests/e2e/dashboard-layout.spec.ts` before the fix and inspect the later failures showing the sign-in page instead of the dashboard.

bugs_found.description: The settings layout E2E spec used an ambiguous `getByText('Exports')` locator that matched the sidebar item, breadcrumb, and page heading.
bugs_found.expected: The placeholder-page assertion should target the page heading specifically.
bugs_found.actual: Playwright strict mode rejected the locator with three matches even though the Exports page rendered correctly.
bugs_found.reproduction: Run `npx playwright test tests/e2e/settings-layout.spec.ts` before the fix and observe the strict mode violation in `placeholder pages render with coming soon message`.

fix_description: Updated the dashboard/settings layout E2E specs to target the actual accessible roles, avoid invalidating shared auth state mid-suite, and use precise placeholder-page assertions; the product shell itself passed the manual and automated QA checks.

tested_steps: Ran `make test` and confirmed the unit suite passed with 1527 tests.
Ran `npx playwright test tests/e2e/smoke.spec.ts` and confirmed all 11 smoke tests passed with the authenticated Playwright setup.
Started the dev server with `PLAYWRIGHT_TEST=true npm run dev` so the live app, test-session route, browser checks, and API verification all exercised the same running environment.

Used Ever CLI on `http://localhost:3015/dashboard/new-project` with a live authenticated session to create `QA Feature 001 Alpha` with a GitHub repo URL, verified the app redirected back to `/dashboard`, and verified the new project became the active project in the sidebar and dashboard shell. Saved screenshots to `ralph/screenshots/qa/feature-001-dashboard-after-create.jpg` and `ralph/screenshots/qa/feature-001-project-selector-open.jpg`.
Used the sidebar project selector in Ever to verify all projects in the org were listed and the active project switched when another project was selected.

Used Ever CLI on `http://localhost:3015/settings/project/general` to update the active project's name and deployment subdomain, observed the `Changes saved` confirmation, refreshed the dashboard, and verified the renamed project and edited subdomain rendered in the shell and site preview. Saved a screenshot to `ralph/screenshots/qa/feature-001-settings-saved.jpg`.
Queried Postgres directly after the settings save and confirmed the edited project row persisted the updated `name`, `subdomain`, and repo URL in the `projects` table.

Created a fresh isolated admin org via `POST /api/test/create-session`, attempted to `DELETE` its only project through `DELETE /api/projects/:id`, and confirmed the live API returned `400 Cannot delete the organization's last project`.

Created a second isolated admin org and verified slug collision handling by creating two `Slug Clash` projects through `POST /api/projects`; the first project used `slug-clash` and the second persisted a suffixed slug `slug-clash-mnqsuezd` with matching subdomains.

Created an `Update Target` project through the API, updated it through `PUT /api/projects/:id`, and confirmed the response plus a direct Postgres query both showed `Update Target Edited` with the edited subdomain and unchanged repo URL.

Created editor and viewer users in the same org through the test-session route plus direct membership inserts, then verified both received `403 Only admins can delete projects` on `DELETE /api/projects/:id` while the admin session deleted the same target project successfully with `200`, and confirmed the project row was removed from Postgres.
Ran `make test` and confirmed the unit suite passed with 1530 tests.
Ran `npx playwright test tests/e2e/smoke.spec.ts` against the Playwright-configured dev server and confirmed all 11 smoke tests passed.

Ran `npx playwright test tests/e2e/onboarding.spec.ts` and confirmed all 16 onboarding checks passed, including GitHub URL validation, refresh resume, GitHub repo persistence, existing-org redirect, and concurrent org creation protection.

Used Ever CLI on `http://localhost:3015/login` with the real Google test account `ashleyha0317@gmail.com`, clicked `Continue with Google`, and verified the live OAuth flow established a real Better Auth session and landed on the authenticated dashboard. Saved evidence to `ralph/screenshots/qa/onboard-001-google-session-dashboard.jpg`.
Navigated the real Google-authenticated browser session to `/onboarding` and verified users who already belong to an org are redirected back to `/dashboard` instead of seeing the wizard.

Created a fresh QA user via the test-session route, then exercised the real onboarding backend sequence with live API calls: `POST /api/orgs`, `POST /api/projects` with `repoUrl=https://github.com/acme/docs` and `createInitialDeployment=true`, `GET /api/projects`, and `GET /api/deployments`.

Queried Postgres for `qa-onboard-1775699930@example.com` and confirmed the onboarding flow persisted exactly one organization, one project with the GitHub repo URL, the derived subdomain `qa-onboard-org-20260409-mnqtw6ax-qa-onboard-docs`, project status `active`, and the initial deployment status `succeeded`.
Ran `make test` and confirmed the unit suite passed with 1534 tests.

Restarted the local dev server in `PLAYWRIGHT_TEST=true` mode so the authenticated Playwright fixture could use the test-session route, then ran `npx playwright test tests/e2e/smoke.spec.ts` and confirmed all 11 smoke tests passed.

Used Ever CLI on `http://localhost:3015/editor/main` with the live authenticated session to verify the editor shell, switch between Navigation and Files, create a nested page at `qa-loop-20260409/deep/leaf`, and confirm the new hierarchy rendered in both views. Saved evidence to `ralph/screenshots/qa/feature-002-editor-saved.jpg`.
Attempted to create the same path again through the create-page modal and confirmed the UI surfaced `A page with this path already exists in this project` without creating a duplicate row.
Selected the new page, switched to Markdown mode, saved non-trivial MDX content, and verified the editor re-rendered the page header/path plus the derived TOC from the saved headings.

Created an isolated API-backed QA session through `POST /api/test/create-session`, verified `POST /api/projects/:id/pages` enforced path uniqueness with `409`, verified `PUT /api/projects/:id/pages/:pageId` persisted content updates, and queried Postgres to confirm the page row stored the expected `path`, `title`, and `content`.
Updated the same page with a 135017 byte MDX payload through the real API and confirmed the save returned `200 OK`, covering the large-content path.

Created a second editor session in the same org via the test-session route plus a direct membership insert, issued overlapping `PUT /api/projects/:id/pages/:pageId` requests from both sessions, and confirmed the final stored content was the later write (`from user2 final`), i.e. last-write-wins with no conflict handling.

Used the live UI delete confirmation to remove `qa-loop-20260409/deep/leaf`, then verified the page disappeared from the file tree and the content area reset to `Select a page to edit`. Saved evidence to `ralph/screenshots/qa/feature-002-editor-after-delete.jpg`.
Ran `make test` and confirmed the unit suite passed with 1538 tests.

Restarted the local dev server in `PLAYWRIGHT_TEST=true` mode so Playwright and live browser QA used the same authenticated-capable environment, then verified `POST /api/test/create-session` returned `200 OK`.
Ran `PLAYWRIGHT_TEST=true npx playwright test tests/e2e/smoke.spec.ts` and confirmed all 11 smoke tests passed.
Ran `PLAYWRIGHT_TEST=true npx playwright test tests/e2e/editor-dual-mode.spec.ts` and confirmed all 9 dual-mode editor tests passed, including the visual-toolbar placeholder insertion check.

Used Ever CLI on `http://localhost:3015/editor/main` with a live authenticated session to open a dedicated QA page, seed rich MDX content, and verify visual mode rendered live heading, bold, italic, and link formatting plus interactive MDX previews for Note, Card, and Tab blocks. Saved evidence to `ralph/screenshots/qa/feature-003-visual-rich-content.jpg`.

Switched the same page from visual mode to markdown mode and confirmed the raw MDX preserved frontmatter, headings, link syntax, Note/Card/Tab component syntax, and later round-tripped back to visual mode without data loss.

Triggered markdown editing through the live textarea, waited for the 2 second debounce, and verified auto-save persisted `### Auto Saved Heading` both through the UI TOC update and through `GET /api/projects/:id/pages/:pageId` returning the saved MDX.

Opened the Add new dropdown in markdown mode, verified the Tab/Dropdown/Anchor/Card/Columns/Language/Product/Version options rendered, inserted a Card snippet at the cursor, waited for auto-save, and confirmed both the textarea content and the saved API payload contained the inserted Card block.

Opened Page settings in Ever, changed the page title, slug, and description through the blur-driven form, and verified the updated values reflected in the sidebar/header, the page API response, and Postgres. Saved evidence to `ralph/screenshots/qa/feature-003-settings-saved.jpg`.

Updated the page to a 568-line MDX document, confirmed visual mode still rendered and generated TOC anchors through Section 550, switched to markdown mode, and verified the line-number gutter scroll stayed exactly in sync with the textarea (`scrollTop` 1200 on both elements). Saved evidence to `ralph/screenshots/qa/feature-003-large-doc-markdown.jpg`.

Queried Postgres directly for the QA page row and confirmed the persisted record stored title `QA Feature 003 Settings Saved`, path `qa/feature-003-settings-saved`, description `Saved via page settings panel`, and `content_length` 27643.
